title: Oldenlandia capitellata
taxonomy: Oldenlandia capitellata belongs to the family Rubiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The genus Oldenlandia is a group of annual or perennial herbs found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Oldenlandia capitellata is native to various parts of Asia, including China and India. It is classified as a member of the tribe Spermaeae within the family Rubiaceae.
uses: Oldenlandia capitellata is traditionally used in some cultures for its medicinal properties, including treatment of fever and various infections. In some regions, it is used to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Additionally, it is believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help soothe irritated skin and reduce swelling. The plant's leaves are often used in traditional medicine, typically made into teas, infusions, or applied topically. More research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Oldenlandia capitellata for medicinal purposes.
